TextView.android:text: Question 1.1 (MCQ): What shall be the form of government?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Constitutional Monarchy (Monarch is ceremonial head of state)
B. Parliamentary Republic (President is ceremonial; Prime Minister governs the state)
C. Presidential Republic (President is both head of state and head of government)
D. Semi-Presidential Republic (President and Prime Minister share executive powers)

TextView.android:text: Question 1.2 (MCQ): What is the source of state authority?

RadioButton.android:text: A. The Monarch by divine right
B. The people, through democratic processes
C. The union of the stats

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Name of the Treaties and Conventions

TextView.android:text: Question 1.3 (Checkbox): Select explicitly stated principles (at least 1)

CheckBox.android:text: The state is secular (freedom of conscience) [Default]
a. The state is social (committed to welfare and reducing inequality)

TextView.android:text: b. The state is based Principles of:

CheckBox.android:text: a1. Nationalism
b1. Independence
c1. Livelihood
d1. fraternity (brotherhood)
e1. Equality

TextView.android:text: CHAPTER TWO: FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS AND DUTIES
all citizens of the [country name] shall have equal rights, freedoms and duties unless suspended by law.
Question 2.0 (MCQ): What is the source of the fundamental rights?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Natural (by birth right non null able)

TextView.android:text: Question 2.1.1 (Checkbox): absolute and non-suspendable rights (Select all):

CheckBox.android:text: A. Right to life (no arbitrary deprivation)
B. Right to personal Liberty
C. Prohibition of ex post facto laws
D. Right to a fair trial before an independent court
E. Prohibition of double jeopardy
F. Protection against self-incrimination.
G. Right to equality before Law.
A. Right to elect and to be elected according to law.
B. Right to privacy
C. Right to work
D. Right to right to rest and leisure
E. Right to material assistance in old age
F. Right to education
G. Right to bring complain and get compensation
H. Right to asylum.
I. Right to own, inherit land and private property
j. Right to lodge written or oral complaints
A. Freedom of speech and expression
B. Freedom of peaceful assembly
C. Freedom of association
D. Freedom of movement and residence
E. Freedom of religion and atheism
F. Freedom of press and publication
G. Freedom of press
H. Freedom of correspondence
I. Freedom of person
J. Freedom of holding processions and demonstrations
K. Freedom to strike
L. Freedom of profession
M. Freedom of research and cultural pursuits
N. Freedom of marriage
O. Freedom from arrest without court order

TextView.android:text: Question 2.3 (MCQ): Who may suspend these freedoms and rights?

CheckBox.android:text: A. Right to life
B. Right to personal Liberty
C. Prohibition of ex post facto laws
D. Right to a fair trial
E. Prohibition of double jeopardy
F. Protection against self-incrimination.
G. Right to equality before Law.
H. Right to elect and to be elected
I. Right to privacy
J. Right to work
K. Right to right to rest and leisure
L. Right to material assistance in old age
M. Right to education
N. Right to bring complain/get compensation
O. Right to asylum.
P. Right to own, inherit land and private property
Q. Right to lodge written or oral complaints
A. Freedom of speech and expression
B. Freedom of peaceful assembly
C. Freedom of association
D. Freedom of movement and residence
E. Freedom of religion and atheism
F. Freedom of press and publication
G. Freedom of press
H. Freedom of correspondence
I. Freedom of person
J. Freedom of holding processions
K. Freedom to strike
L. Freedom of profession
M. Freedom of research and cultural pursuits
N. Freedom of marriage
O. Freedom from arrest without court order

TextView.android:text: Question 2.5 (Checkbox): Shall the constitution include Fundamental Duties?

RadioButton.android:text: No, duties shall not be enumerated

CheckBox.android:text: A. To abide by the constitution and respect its ideals
B. To defend the country and render service
C. To pay taxes in accordance with law
D. To promote harmony and brotherhood
E. To protect and preserve the environment
G. To respect and protect public property

TextView.android:text: Question 3.1 (MCQ): How shall the national flag be determined?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Described in detail within the constitution
B. Determined by law passed by legislature

TextView.android:text: Question 3.2 (MCQ): How shall the national emblem be determined?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Described in detail within the constitution
B. Determined by law passed by legislature

TextView.android:text: Question 3.3 (MCQ): The capital city shall be:

RadioButton.android:text: A. Designated by name in the constitution
B. Determined by law passed by legislature

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Enter name of the capital city

TextView.android:text: CHAPTER FOUR: DIRECTIVE PRINCIPLES OF STATE POLICY
Question 4.1 (Checkbox): guided by which non-justiciable principles? (Select at least 3):

CheckBox.android:text: A. Ensure equitable distribution of wealth
B. Provide free and compulsory education
C. Secure living wage and decent conditions
D. Promote international peace
E. Protect and improve the environment
F. Promote cottage industries in rural areas
G. Separate the judiciary from the executive

TextView.android:text: CHAPTER FIVE: STRUCTURE OF THE STATE
Question 5.1 (MCQ): Structure of the national legislature?
Third house consists of all elected members of lower and upper house. Only power is to enact new constitution with 2/3 majority.
Question 5.2 (MCQ): How shall members of the lower house be elected?
Question 5.3 (Checkbox): Powers legislature exclusively hold? (Select all):

CheckBox.android:text: A. Power to declare war
B. Power to ratify treaties
C. Power to approve the national budget
D. Power to confirm major executive appointments
E. Power to impeach the President

RadioButton.android:text: A. indirect election by lower house

CheckBox.android:text: A. appeal to reconsideration of laws
B. Power to ratify treaties
C. Power to suggest laws/amendments
D. Power to elect a president

TextView.android:text: Question 5.6 (MCQ): How shall the President be selected?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Direct election by popular vote
B. Election by an electoral college

TextView.android:text: Question 5.7 (MCQ): Max number of terms for President?

RadioButton.android:text: A. Direct election by the people
B. Appointed by President (must command confidence)
D. Elected directly by the legislature

TextView.android:text: Question 5.8 (cont MCQ): duration of term for president/MP?

RadioButton.android:text: B. Appointed by President with legislature consent
C. Appointed by an independent Commission

CheckBox.android:text: A. Life tenure or until retirement age
B. Salaries cannot be reduced during term
C. Removed only by special process (impeachment)
D. Administrative and financial autonomy

TextView.android:text: The judiciary can review all laws, executive actions, and administrative decisions for constitutionality

RadioButton.android:text: A. The President (civilian Head of State)
D. A senior military officer designated by law

CheckBox.android:text: A. No active military personnel may hold civil office
B. Power to declare war rests with legislature
C. No political party may use armed forces
A. Free movement of goods
B. State ownership of natural resources
C. Regulation of private monopolies
D. Support for cooperative enterprises
A. Free and compulsory primary education
C. Educational institutions free from political interference
D. Private educational institutions shall be permitted

TextView.android:text: Question 8.2 (MCQ): Minimum budget allocated to education?

RadioButton.android:text: B. At least 2% of national budget
C. At least 5% of national budget
D. At least 8% of national budget

TextView.android:text: Question 9.1 (Checkbox): Protections for minority groups? (Select all):

CheckBox.android:text: A. Right to conserve language and culture
B. Protection from discrimination
C. Special representation in legislature
D. Autonomy for regions

RadioButton.android:text: A. To amend the constitution simple mejority in parliment with president approval is needed
B. To amend the constitution supreme mejority in parliment is needed
C. To amend the constitution supreme mejority in parliment with a national referandum to be conduct to get the approval of the people
D. To amend the constitution supreme mejority in parliment with a state simple majority of states accepting the amendment
